code_blocks_over_limit.heading_text: Example SAML assertion for identity provider group mapping

chunks.body:
This guide explains how a new customer of Security Cloud Control signs on, creates an enterprise, uses a claim code to integrate their applications
with Security Cloud Control, creates user roles and user groups, and onboards firewall and other devices.
It is intended for Security Cloud Control administrators with full access to the organization.

chunks.source_title: Overview of Cisco Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Overview of Cisco Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
Cisco Security Cloud Control is a security platform that allows you to manage your security products and achieve
security outcomes from a single integrated interface.
Integrating security products in the platform is a streamlined experience. After
purchasing your subscriptions to Cisco security products, you receive a single email,
with a single claim code for all the subscriptions you purchased. Entering the claim
code in your new Security Cloud Control organization provisions all your products to
Security Cloud Control simultaneously.
Within Security Cloud Control, user and group management occurs at the platform level.
Roles are assigned to these users and groups to define their privileges for
administering Security Cloud Control and the integrated products.

chunks.source_title: Overview of Cisco Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Overview of Cisco Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
Navigation between products and tools is intuitive and standardized with a common
platform menu and toolbar for all integrated products.
Security Cloud Control provides these additional core services to all integrated products
on the platform:
Platform Management: Common services such as managing role-based access control, claiming
subscriptions and standardized regional deployment of product instances are
provided by Security Cloud Control. By centralizing these functions, Security Cloud Control ensures a consistent user experience in provisioning and managing access
across all Cisco security products managed from the platform. Administrators
reach these common services from the Platform Management menu in the main navigation bar of Security Cloud Control.

chunks.source_title: Overview of Cisco Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Overview of Cisco Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
AI Assistant: The Cisco AI Assistant in Security Cloud Control is designed to streamline security operations by providing AI-driven
insights, automation, and contextual guidance. It assists administrators in
managing security policies, troubleshooting issues, and optimizing
configurations across Cisco’s security products, including Firewall, Duo, , and Secure Access. By
leveraging natural language processing and cross-platform intelligence, the
assistant enhances efficiency, accelerates incident response, and simplifies
security workflows.
Global Search: The ability to search for values across
products in the platform.
Shared Objects: Creating and managing objects that can be
shared across devices and policies.
Unified documentation portal: A documentation "Help"

chunks.source_title: Overview of Cisco Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Overview of Cisco Security Cloud Control
chunks.body: experience where all documentation is accessible in one portal.

chunks.source_title: Overview of Cisco Security Cloud Control
heading_path: Overview of Cisco Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Products You Can Integrate with Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
From Security Cloud Control, you can manage all of these security products:
AI Defense
Security Cloud Control Firewall Management
Multicloud Defense
Secure Access
Secure Workload

chunks.source_title: Overview of Cisco Security Cloud Control
heading_path: Overview of Cisco Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Products You Can Launch from Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
From Security Cloud Control, you can launch these security products. After launch, these products operate as standalone products and you cannot manage them through Security Cloud Control. You can only claim or deactivate the licenses of such products from Security Cloud Control.
In the Security Cloud Control toolbar, click the nine-dot menu to launch these products:
Cisco Secure Email Threat Defense
Cisco Secure Endpoint
Cisco Duo
Cisco XDR

chunks.source_title: Products and Solutions Supported by Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Products and Solutions Supported by Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
Currently, these are the products you can
integrate with Security Cloud Control.
AI Defense: AI Defense addresses risks for users and providers of AI. Using network visibility and
enforcement points in the Security Cloud Control, AI Defense adds detection and enforcement measures to discover sanctioned and unsanctioned
AI workloads, applications, models, data, and user access across your distributed
cloud environment. For organizations that develop and deliver AI-powered services,
AI Defense detects vulnerabilities in your AI models before they're delivered. For your
running AI applications, AI Defense guardrails intercept rapidly evolving threats,
including prompt injections, denial of service, and data leakage.See AI Defense Documentation for more
information.

chunks.source_title: Products and Solutions Supported by Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Products and Solutions Supported by Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
SASE Management: SASE Management is a cloud-delivered security solution designed to simplify and unify your network
and security policy management. SASE Management enables seamless management of Secure Internet Access (SIA) and Secure Private Access
(SPA) for your Catalyst SD-WAN infrastructure and device policy with Cisco Secure Access cloud access rules in a single, integrated platform. If SASE Management is enabled on your organization, you can share some network object and network object
groups between Cisco Secure Access and Catalyst SD-WAN security policies. See the Cisco SASE Management Administration Guide and Common Objects and Object Groups for more
information.

chunks.source_title: Products and Solutions Supported by Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Products and Solutions Supported by Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
Security Cloud Control Firewall Management: Security Cloud Control Firewall Management (formerly Cisco Defense Orchestrator) is a cloud-based security policy
manager that simplifies and unifies policy across your Cisco firewalls and other
devices. See Firewall in Security Cloud Control
Documentation for more information.
Multicloud Defense: Multicloud Defense provides a simplified and highly automated approach to multicloud security. This
solution allows organizations to manage and secure their multicloud environments
using a single SaaS delivered control plane, and centralized or distributed
PaaS-delivered data plane architectures. Multicloud Defense provides continuous visibility, unified protection and dynamic policy updates

chunks.source_title: Products and Solutions Supported by Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Products and Solutions Supported by Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
across all major cloud providers, thereby eliminating the need for separate point
solutions for solutions for each cloud provider. See Multicloud Defense Documentation for more
information.
Secure Access: Cisco Secure Access is a cloud-based platform that provides multiple levels of defense against
internet-based threats. Connect securely to the internet, SaaS apps, and private
digital resources from your organization's network or roaming off-network. Using
policy rules, configure and enforce security controls on collections of resources,
users, and devices. See Secure Access Documentation for more

chunks.source_title: Products and Solutions Supported by Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Products and Solutions Supported by Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
information. Secure Access subscriptions also include the Identity Intelligence integration via Security Cloud Control at no additional charge—this does not include access to the standalone Identity Intelligence dashboard. For more information, see Integrate Cisco Identity Intelligence with Secure
Access.
Secure Workload: Cisco Secure Workload (formerly Tetration) seamlessly delivers zero-trust micro-segmentation across any
workload, environment, or location from a single console. With comprehensive
visibility into every workload interaction and powerful AI/ML-driven automation, Secure Workload reduces the attack surface by preventing lateral movement, identifies workload
behavior anomalies, helps rapidly remediate threats, and continuously monitors

chunks.source_title: Products and Solutions Supported by Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Products and Solutions Supported by Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
policy compliance. See Secure Workload Documentation for more
information.
Cisco XDR (Extended Detection and Response): Cisco XDR, is a cloud-native, extensible security solution designed to simplify security
operations and accelerate incident response. It collects and analyzes telemetry from
multiple sources—including endpoint, network, firewall, email, identity, and DNS—to
detect sophisticated threats with enriched context and threat intelligence from Cisco
Talos.
Cisco XDR prioritizes threats based on potential risk and impact, reduces false positives, and
provides AI-driven guidance and automation to streamline investigations and remediation.
This unified approach enables Security Operations Center (SOC) teams to act faster,

chunks.source_title: Products and Solutions Supported by Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Products and Solutions Supported by Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
improve productivity, and build security resilience by integrating both Cisco and
third-party security tools in a multi-vector, multi-vendor environment. See Cisco XDR Help Center for more information.

chunks.source_title: Browser Settings for Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Browser Settings for Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
You can access Security Cloud Control using Single Sign-On through your organization’s identity provider (IdP).
To ensure a seamless and secure login process, your browser must allow cookies from these
domains:
webex.com: Cisco account leverages select technology and decades of security
expertise from Cisco and Webex to deliver a new, secure, and seamless login
experience across Cisco platforms. Cisco account does not grant access to Webex
products but because of the underlying technology, you may be required to allow
cookies from the webex.com domain during login.
cisco.com
These cookies are necessary to:
Maintain your login session securely.
Enable core functionality and personalized settings.
Support authentication flows and access to collaboration tools.

chunks.source_title: Browser Settings for Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Browser Settings for Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
Provide enhanced performance and troubleshooting capabilities.
Blocking or disabling cookies from these domains may result in login issues, interrupted
sessions, or degraded user experience.

Before you begin your initial setup of Security Cloud Control, make sure you have ordered one of its managed products through the Cisco Commerce
web site and you have received your welcome email with claim code.
This is the workflow you will follow to get started with Security Cloud Control.
Sign in to Security Cloud Control using your Security Cloud Sign On Account. You can create an account if you
don't have one.
Create an organization and define a preferred region for that organization.
Enter the claim code you received in the Security Cloud Control welcome email in order to claim subscriptions to your product instances.
Activate product instances.
Now that you have created your organization and claimed your subscription, choose
your home page.
Add a domain to your organization.

Integrate the identity provider with Secure Cloud Sign On.
Create users and groups; assign them roles for precise product management and
control.
Onboard firewalls and other devices to the Firewall application in Security Cloud
Control.
Continue with the next steps of configuring your organization.

chunks.source_title: Sign in to Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Sign in to Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
This chapter explains Cisco Secure Sign On, how to create an account if you need to, and
signing in to Security Cloud Control.

chunks.source_title: Overview of Security Cloud Sign On
chunks.heading_text: Overview of Security Cloud Sign On
chunks.body:
With Cisco Security Cloud Sign On, you can easily access many Cisco Security products with one set of credentials from
any device. Once you sign in with your username and password, all your Cisco Security
products are displayed as applications in your customizable dashboard.
Click an application and you're automatically signed-in, for seamless workflows
across your Cisco Security products. You no longer have to remember and juggle
multiple passwords.
Integration with Duo's Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) means adaptive, layered,
and simplified authentication. One push notification, one tap, instant
access.
Optionally, use Security Cloud Control to integrate your own identity provider
(IdP) with Security Cloud Sign On.

chunks.source_title: Products Supported by Security Cloud Sign On
chunks.heading_text: Products Supported by Security Cloud Sign On
chunks.body:
This guide lists Cisco security products that support Security Cloud Sign On. Some products support Security Cloud Sign On by default and require no configuration changes. Other Cisco security products require you to opt-in to Security Cloud Sign On or migrate your users.
For each product listed below it's indicated if Security Cloud Sign On is enabled by default or requires you to opt-in or migrate your users. For products that require opt-in or user migration, links are provided to the relevant documentation.
Product
Opt-in required?
Documentation
Cisco Cloudlock
Yes
Opt-in guide
Cisco Secure Firewall
No
N/A
Cisco Meraki
Yes
Opt-in guide
Cisco Secure Access
No
Secure Access Single Sign-On Authentication
Cisco Secure Cloud Analytics (formerly Stealthwatch Cloud)
Yes
Migration guide

chunks.source_title: Products Supported by Security Cloud Sign On
chunks.heading_text: Products Supported by Security Cloud Sign On
chunks.body:
Cisco Secure Email Threat Defense (formerly Cloud Mailbox)
No
N/A

No

chunks.source_title: Creating a Security Cloud Sign On Account
chunks.heading_text: Creating a Security Cloud Sign On Account
chunks.body:
To create a Security Cloud Sign On account, you need to provide an email address where the account activation email will be sent. Every Security Cloud Sign On user account is required to use multifactor authentication (MFA). Duo MFA is included with Security Cloud Sign On account at no charge.
If you already have a Security Cloud Sign on account, see Signing in to Security Cloud Control.

chunks.source_title: Creating a Security Cloud Sign On Account
heading_path: Creating a Security Cloud Sign On Account
chunks.body:
1
Open the Account Sign Up
page.
2
Enter the requested information, agree to the end-user license agreement, and click Sign up.
An activation email is sent to the address you provided.
3
Locate the "Activate Account" email from Cisco and click Activate Account.
The activation link expires in 7 days.
4
Select a Duo multifactor authentication option (Touch, Duo Mobile, Security Key, or Phone Number) and complete the verification process. See Duo Guide to MFA and Device Enrollment for help.
5
Optionally, add additional authentication factors, or click Skip for now.
6
Click Log in with Duo and sign in using your preferred
authentication option.
7
Click Finish to finish sign in.

chunks.source_title: Signing in to Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Signing in to Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
To sign in to Security Cloud Control, you need a Cisco Security Cloud
Sign On account. If you don’t have an account, you can create one using Creating a Security Cloud Sign On Account and configure multifactor authentication with Duo MFA.
If you have only one organization associated with your account, it is always the default account when you log in. If you have multiple organizations that are associated with your account, the one that you used last is selected after you sign in.
Be sure your browser is configured to allow cookies from cisco.com and webex.com. See
Browser Settings for Security Cloud Control.

chunks.source_title: Signing in to Security Cloud Control
heading_path: Signing in to Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
1
Open Security Cloud Control at https://security.cisco.com/.
2
Sign in with your Security Cloud Sign On credentials and MFA options that you established when creating your account.
This chapter describes what a Security Cloud Controlorganization is and the importance of the cloud computing region you create that organization in.

You can create multiple organizations, each with their own set of users, products, and other organization data.
For a visual walkthrough of this process, watch the video Create an Organization.

1
In Security Cloud Control, hover your cursor over the Organization menu at the top of the browser and click Create new
organization.
Note that by creating a new organization, you exit the current organization.

2
Enter the name of the organization.

3
Set the preferred region for the organization.
From the Region drop-down list, select a preferred region or a country. (The Region drop-down list displays a list of major geographical regions that are available for hosting the cloud deployments.)
The region that you select in this step determines the deployable regions for all the products and their services that are associated with this organization.
After you set a region preference for the organization, you can't change it. We recommend that you do not adjust the region of the existing organization. However, if you must do so, contact Cisco Technical Assistance. Alternatively, you can create a new organization for an additional subscription with a different region preference.

Changing the region preference could impact the existing product deployments and data retention.
Based on the preferred region for the organization, Security Cloud Control assigns a deployment region for a product. If the product does not
directly support the preferred region, Security Cloud Control makes a best effort (based on a hierarchy logic) to align the available
product deployable region with the preferred region. If the available
deployment regions for the product do not match the preferred region, the
product is assigned a region based on its available deployment options. For
more information about setting your region, see Setting a Region for an
Organization.

4
Click Create.
Security Cloud Control creates a new organization and reloads the page.

This chapter provides an overview of the subscription process, how to claim your product
subscriptions, and onboard new products onto Security Cloud Control.

When you purchase a new subscription from Cisco, a welcome email with the subscription claim code is automatically sent to the Provisioning Contact specified during the purchasing process. If you included the End Customer contact during the purchase, this contact also receives a copy of the initial welcome email. The welcome email is sent on the requested start date of the subscription.
For Enterprise Agreement subscriptions, the welcome email and claim code are sent when the first request is made within the Enterprise Agreement Workspace (EAWS), not on the requested subscription start date. The welcome email and claim code for Enterprise Agreement are sent to the technical contact who makes the request within the EAWS.

A Security Cloud Control administrator uses the Claim subscription (1) option to claim the
subscription for the current organization.
After a subscription is claimed, its products are listed in the Product and service activation status section, with their corresponding start dates (2). The organization administrator clicks Action required (3) to activate the product. Activated products are listed in the Products section (4).
An organization supports only one active instance of a product. See Single Product Instance in an
Organization.
For each activated product, the product subscription details are displayed as a separate tile.
Trial products display a label. Externally managed product instances that have been attached to Security Cloud also display an label.

If there are any modifications to your subscription, such as changes to a product, tier, quantity, or the addition of a shared add-on, within the same subscription, such changes are automatically processed and updated in your active product instance. The administrator and all contacts that are specified during the purchase process receive email notifications about subscription updates.
If you have purchased Security Cloud Control Firewall Management but do not yet have your subscription code, and your new organization has been created, enable the Firewall application by navigating
Platform Management > Product Subscriptions > Subscriptions and in the banner that appears on the Subscriptions page, click Enable Firewall now.

chunks.source_title: Enable Security Cloud Control Firewall Management
chunks.heading_text: Enable Security Cloud Control Firewall Management
chunks.body:
Your organization is entitled to Security Cloud Control Firewall Management. You can enable the Firewall Management capabilities either by claiming a subscription for it or through an enable option on the Security Cloud Control UI.
Use this procedure to enable Security Cloud Control Firewall Management if you do not have a subscription for it.

chunks.source_title: Enable Security Cloud Control Firewall Management
heading_path: Enable Security Cloud Control Firewall Management
chunks.body:
1
Sign on to your Security Cloud Control account and open your new organization.
2
Select
Platform Management > Product Subscriptions > Subscriptions.
3
In the banner that appears on the Subscriptions page, click Enable Firewall Management now.

chunks.source_title: Enable Security Cloud Control Firewall Management
chunks.body: Verify that Firewall appears in the Products section.

chunks.source_title: Enable Multicloud Defense in Security Cloud Control
chunks.heading_text: Enable Multicloud Defense in Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
Your Security Cloud Control organization is entitled to Multicloud Defense. You can enable Multicloud Defense either by claiming a Multicloud Defense subscription or through an enable option on the Security Cloud Control UI.
Use this procedure to enable Multicloud Defense if you do not have a subscription claim code.

chunks.source_title: Enable Multicloud Defense in Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
Ensure that you have enabled Security Cloud Control Firewall Management.
You must have an active Security Cloud Control Firewall Management to enable Multicloud Defense in Security Cloud Control.

chunks.source_title: Enable Multicloud Defense in Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
1
Sign in to your Security Cloud Control account and open your new organization.
2
Select
Platform Management > Product Subscriptions > Subscriptions.
3
In the banner that appears on the Subscriptions page, click Enable Multicloud Defense now.
Multicloud Defense Controller is enabled for your organization.

Your Security Cloud Control organization is entitled to Mesh Policy Engine. You can enable Mesh Policy Engine by claiming a subscription or by using the Enable option in the Security Cloud Control UI.
The Enable Mesh Policy Engine option is intended for use with Cisco firewalls and devices. If you have third-party firewalls and devices that you want to manage through Mesh Policy Engine, you must purchase the Mesh Policy Engine subscription.
If you claim a Mesh Policy Engine subscription within an organization that already has Mesh Policy Engine enabled through the Enable option, your license entitlements are automatically applied to your existing Mesh Policy Engine instance.

If you have a subscription for Mesh Policy Engine, complete the steps in the Claim a Subscription procedure to activate the service.
If you enabled Firewall Management using the Enable Firewall option in Security Cloud Control, you cannot enable Mesh Policy Engine. In such a case, to enable Mesh Policy Engine, you must purchase a subscription for Mesh Policy Engine or a subscription for Firewall Management .
If you have already activated Security Cloud Control Firewall Management, follow this procedure to enable Mesh Policy Engine.
To enable Mesh Policy Engine in Security Cloud Control, you must have an active subscription-based license for Security Cloud Control Firewall Management.

1
Sign in to Security Cloud Control and select your organization.
2
Select
Platform Management > Product Subscriptions > Subscriptions.
3
In the notifications banner that appears on the Subscriptions page, click Enable Mesh Policy Engine.
The Mesh Policy Engine service is enabled for your organization.

An administrator uses a claim code to claim the subscription for the organization.
For Enterprise Agreement subscriptions, the welcome email and the claim code are sent when the first request is made within the Enterprise Agreement Workspace (EAWS), not at the requested start date of the subscription. These are sent to the technical contact who makes the request within the EAWS.
After a subscription is claimed, the subscription’s products are added to the Product and service activation status list.
For a visual walkthrough of this subscription claim process, watch the video Claim a Subscription.

You require a subscription claim code to complete these steps.
An organization supports only one instance of a product. For more information, see Single Product Instance in an
Organization.

1
Sign in to your Security Cloud Control account and open the organization in which you want to claim and activate the products in the subscription.

2
3
Choose
Product Subscriptions > Subscriptions

4
Click Claim subscription in the upper-right corner.

5
Enter the claim code and click Next.

6
Select the product instances to which you want to attach the subscription licenses. You can associate an existing instance or let the system create a new instance.
To apply the subscription license to a new instance of the product, choose Create new instance from the drop-down list in the Create new instance or attach existing column.
After the subscription claim is successful, a new instance of the product is created and the subscription license is applied to this new instance.
To apply the subscription license to an existing instance of the product, choose Attach existing instance from the drop-down list in the Create new instance or attach existing column.

You may need to take additional steps to attach the license to an existing instance. Contact the product team or the activation specialists to begin the license application process.
If the organization has an active instance of the product, there can't be another instance in the same organization. Claim the subscription in a separate organization (new or existing) to activate another instance of the product.
If a trial license of a product is active and you claim a subscription for that product, you are prompted to apply the license on the trial instance. On your confirmation, the subscription is automatically applied to the trial instance and the trial license is canceled.

If your subscription has a trial license for an active instance of a product, you can apply the trial subscription to the product instance.
The product gets the entitlements of the trial subscription and is tagged as "Trial".
See Activating a trial license on an active product instance for more information.
If you have attached an external instance of the product, the subscription license is automatically applied to the external instance.

7
Review the list of products, their deployment regions, and then click Claim.
A deployment region is assigned to each product instance. The icons that are associated with the Deployments indicate how a deployment region for a product instance aligns with the selected region.
The following table describes the values that you see under the Deployments column.
Deployment
Description
Region will be per existing tenant
If you chose to attach the license to an existing instance, the Deployments column displays "Region will be per existing tenant".

This means the deployment region matches the region of the existing product instance. If the region of the existing product does not align with the preferred region of the organization, we recommend that you cancel this claim and create a new organization with a region that aligns with your existing instance.
Indicates that the selected region or country matches or is similar to the supported product deployments.
For example, organization region is "NAM" and product region is either "NAM" or "US".
Indicates that the product is deployable as Global or that the preferred region aligns with the existing product.
For example, organization region is "NAM" and product region is either "Global" or "Region per existing tenant".

Indicates that the product cannot be deployed in the selected region, so it is assigned a region where it is available.
For example, organization region is "APJ" and product region is "US".
The administrator who claims the subscription is assigned the role of an initial administrator for all products that are claimed in the organization.
You can add more administrators as needed by assigning roles to users.
New product instances are automatically provisioned and added to the list of products on the Subscriptions page.

chunks.source_title: Activate Cisco Duo and Cisco Identity Intelligence Services
chunks.heading_text: Activate Cisco Duo and Cisco Identity Intelligence Services
chunks.body:
You can use the Cisco Duo and Cisco Identity Intelligence services with a Cisco Secure Access or a Cisco XDR subscription.
Use this procedure to activate Duo with Identity Intelligence and utilize its basic features. To learn which features are available, see the
Limited Use Cisco Duo Licenses
details guide.
To ensure that Duo and Identity Intelligence services are available, it is important that the correct Duo administrator (Duo account owner) is selected during the activation process. See Step 4c in this procedure.

chunks.source_title: Activate Cisco Duo and Cisco Identity Intelligence Services
chunks.body:
Ensure that Cisco XDR or Cisco Secure Access is active in your Security Cloud Control organization.
Read this guidance before activating Duo:
Select the initial administrator carefully during activation. Before activating Duo and Cisco Identity Intelligence services, review the guidance in the callout box on the activation pane. This messaging explains how to select or invite the correct Duo owner as the initial administrator.
Activation failures and resets occur most often when you select the wrong administrator.
If the correct Duo administrator is unavailable, use the guidance to invite the correct admin before proceeding. If you ignore this guidance, the activation process stops.

chunks.source_title: Activate Cisco Duo and Cisco Identity Intelligence Services
chunks.body:
To correct your selection of initial administrator, your activation is reset. Review the guidance to activate your Duo and Cisco Identity Intelligence successfully.
Once a Duo instance is activated in a Security Cloud Control organization, it cannot be reused or attached to a different organization.

chunks.source_title: Activate Cisco Duo and Cisco Identity Intelligence Services
chunks.body:
1
Sign in to your Security Cloud Control account and open your organization in which you have claimed Cisco XDR or Cisco Secure Access.

2
Select
Platform Management > Product Subscriptions > Subscriptions.
On the Subscriptions page, you will see a banner for Cisco Duo+Identity Intelligence.

3
Click Action required.

chunks.source_title: Activate Cisco Duo and Cisco Identity Intelligence Services
chunks.body:
4
In the slide-in pane:
If you already have a Duo account that you want to use to activate your Duo and Cisco Identity Intelligence services in y organization, select Connect an existing account. Choose an account from the drop-down list.
To create a new account, select Create a new account.
From the Initial administrator drop-down, select an administrator that you want be the owner of the account created for Duo and Cisco Identity Intelligence service.
If current Duo administrator does not appear in the Initial administrator drop-down list, add the Duo administrator to the Security Cloud Control Administrators by
sending a user invite
. After adding the Duo administrator, select the administrator from the Initial administrator drop-down list.

chunks.source_title: Activate Cisco Duo and Cisco Identity Intelligence Services
chunks.body:
To activate Duo on an existing instance after selecting Connect an existing account, choose a Duo product owner in Security Cloud Control as the initial administrator. This product owner must already own the Duo instance you intend to activate. Activation fails if the selected administrator is not the owner of the existing Duo tenant.
To activate a new Duo instance after selecting Create a new account, select a Duo product owner who is not already an owner of another Duo instance. The selected administrator becomes the owner of the newly created Duo tenant in Security Cloud Control.
Wrong Selection (Reset):

chunks.source_title: Activate Cisco Duo and Cisco Identity Intelligence Services
chunks.body:
If you try to activate a new Duo instance with an existing owner or choose a Duo owner who does not match the existing instance, Security Cloud Control resets the activation process and displays corrective guidance. The guidance explains why your selection did not work and tells you what steps you can take to correct and successfully activate Duo your account.
You see these error messages when you select an incorrect administrator:
Figure 1: Existing administrator selected to activate a new Duo instance
Figure 2: New administrator selected for an existing Duo account
Click Activate.
Duo with Identity Intelligence is now active for your organization. You can choose Identity Intelligence from the Platform Services section in the Security Cloud Control menu.

Cisco's Secure Access Service Edge (SASE) Management integrates Cisco Secure Access and
Cisco SD-WAN Cloud on the Security Cloud Control platform to provide a unified platform for
security and networking.
To onboard SASE Management to Security Cloud Control, complete these steps.
Make sure you have a claim code for a SASE-eligible version of Secure Access and that you have not claimed the subscription yet.

1
Sign on to your Security Cloud Control account and open your organization where you want to onboard SASE Management.

2
3
Choose
Product Subscriptions > Subscriptions.

4
Click Claim subscription in the upper-right corner.

5
Enter the claim code and click Next.

6
In the Review products page, for the Secure Access product, select Create new instance from the drop-down list.
Currently, SASE management is supported only with a new instance of Secure Access.

7
Click Enable SASE Management.

8
In the slide-in pane:
Check the Enable SASE Management check box.
To import all SD-WAN netadmin users with the administrator role in SASE Management, check the Assign the SASE Administrators role to users with the netadmin role in SD-WAN Cloud check box.
If this option remains unchecked, only the user who claims the subscription receives the SASE Administrator role.
Click Enable.

9
In the Review products page, click Next to review your subscription.

10
In the Review subscription page, click Claim.

11
When prompted, click Claim to confirm the subscription claim process.

12
Monitor the notification on the Subscriptions page to check the onboarding status.
A success notification indicates that Security Cloud Control automatically connects to your available SD-WAN instance and enables the SASE Management service with Secure Access.
If the message Unable to find and configure an SD-WAN Cloud instance (formerly cloud-delivered Catalyst SD-WAN) instance. To connect an existing instance, copy the activation code and complete the connection in the SD-WAN Cloud Portal appears, onboard the SD-WAN cloud instance using an activation code.

13
(Optional) If you are unable to access an SD-WAN fabric or want to cancel the SASE Management service, click Cancel activation code.
When prompted, click Cancel code to confirm the cancellation of SASE Management.
After you cancel the activation, Security Cloud Control provisions Secure Access without SASE Management.
Contact Cisco Support if you want to enable SASE Management at a later date.

14
To connect your SD-WAN Cloud instance and activate the SASE Management service, complete these steps:
Copy the SD-WAN Cloud activation code from the Products and service activation status pane of Security Cloud Control.
Go to the SD-WAN Cloud application portal and apply the activation code on the appropriate SD-WAN fabric instance. For detailed steps, refer to Onboard SD-WAN Cloud to Security Cloud Control.
After the SD-WAN fabric is onboarded to Security Cloud Control, you can access it through
Security Cloud Control > Products > Catalyst SD-WAN.
After onboarding, the SASE Management service is available in Security Cloud Control under
Security Cloud Control > Services > SASE Management.

SASE Management service is onboarded to Security Cloud Control and you can access this service from
Security Cloud Control > Services > SASE Management.
All existing SD-WAN Cloud users and their roles are migrated to Security Cloud Control.
You can now manage your SASE solution from the Security Cloud Control platform.

chunks.heading_text: Onboard SD-WAN Fabric to Security Cloud Control
chunks.body: To onboard an SD-WAN fabric to Security Cloud Control, complete these steps:
Make sure you have the activation code to activate SD-WAN fabric in Security Cloud Control.

1
In the SD-WAN Cloud portal, choose
Catalyst Manager > SD-WAN Portal > SASE Integration > Fabrics yet to be onboarded.
2
Select the fabric you want to onboard and click Actions at the top right.
3
Select Activate code and then choose one of the following options:
Option 1: Select the activation code from the list and click Onboard.
Option 2: If you do not see your activation code listed, enter the activation code manually in the Enter Activation Code field, then click Onboard.
The SD-WAN fabric is now onboarded to Security Cloud Control and can be managed in the Security Cloud Control interface.

The first page you see after signing in to Security Cloud Control is your landing page.
If you have not enabled any products in your organization, the dashboard displays
clickable tiles that suggest different configuration tasks for you to perform to setup
your organization.
If your Security Cloud Control organization has a single product associated with it, your default landing page is
the dashboard for that product.
If your Security Cloud Control organization has more than one product associated with it, and one of those products
is Firewall; your landing page is a unified dashboard with access to various monitoring
widgets that you can arrange on the screen.
If you have enabled multiple products in your organization, but none of them are

Firewall, the dashboard displays clickable tiles that suggest different configuration
tasks for you to perform.
After you enable some products on your Security Cloud Control organization, you can also pick your own default landing page.
This is an optional procedure that allows you to set your preferred landing page.

1
At the top of the Security Cloud Control home page or any of your product dashboards, click Set default
home page.
2
Select the product dashboard you want to see first after signing on to your organization.
3
Click Save.

Add and verify a domain for your organization. This is a prerequisite to integrating an identity provider with Security Cloud Sign On. It's also required to enable the organization administrators to reset users’ passwords or MFA settings in the claimed domain.
You can currently verify a single domain with Security Cloud Control. If you must verify multiple domains, open a case with Cisco Technical Assistance Center (Cisco TAC).
To complete this task, you should be able to create a Domain Name System (DNS) record on the registrar service for your domain.

1
2
Choose
Integrations > Domains.

3
In the Domains page, click + Add domain.

4
In the Add New Domain page, enter the domain name you want to claim and click Next.
The Verification page shows the name under Record Name, and the value under Value of a text record that you must create on your domain registrar.

5
In a new browser tab, sign in to your domain name registrar service.

6
Create a new TXT record with the specified Record name and Value provided by Security Cloud Control.

7
Save your changes and allow time for the DNS record to propagate.

8
Return to the Add New Domain page and click Verify.
If the verification is unsuccessful try the following:
Wait a while longer for the DNS record to propagate.
Verify that the type, name, and value of the DNS record you created on your domain registrar matches the values that are generated by Security Cloud Control.
After Security Cloud Control verifies the domain, you can delete the DNS record that you created earlier.

After you've verified your email domain, you can do the following:
Integrate your identity provider with Security Cloud Sign On.
Reset passwords and MFA settings for the users in the claimed domain.

Integrating your identity provider with Security Cloud Sign On requires the following:
A verified email domain to securely route the users to their identity provider for authentication
The ability to create and configure SAML applications in your identity provider's management portal

In response to a SAML authentication request from Security Cloud Sign On, your identity provider sends a SAML response. If the user authenticated successfully, the response includes a SAML assertion that contains the
NameID
,
entityID
, and other user attributes. The SAML response must meet specific criteria, as explained below.

The SAML assertion in the response from your identity provider must contain the following attribute names. These names must be mapped to the corresponding attributes of the identity provider’s user profile. IdP user profile attribute names vary by vendor.

The SAML assertion in the response from your identity provider must contain the following attribute names. These names must be mapped to the corresponding attributes of the IdP’s user profile. IdP user profile attribute names vary by vendor.
SAML assertion attribute name
Identity provider user attribute
firstName
User's first or given name.
lastName
User's last name or surname.
email
User's email. This must match the value of the <NameID> element in the SAML response (see below).
SamlIDPUserGroups
Group memberships of users
The SAML assertion includes an Entity ID, also known as the Audience URI. Ensure that this Entity ID matches the Entity ID that you provide during the SAML metadata configuration.

The value of the
<NameID>
element in the SAML response must be a valid email address and match the value of the assertion’s
email
attribute. The
<NameID>
element's format attribute must be set to one of the following:
urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress
urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:unspecified

The following XML is an example of a SAML response from an identity provider to the Security Cloud Sign On ACL URL. Note that jsmith@example.com is the value of the
<NameID>
element and the
email
SAML response attribute.
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<saml2:Assertion ID="id9538389495975029849262425" IssueInstant="2023-08-02T01:13:04.861Z" Version="2.0"
xmlns:saml2="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:assertion">
<saml2:Issuer Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:entity"/>
<saml2:Subject>
<saml2:NameID Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:unspecified">jsmith@example.com</saml2:NameID>
<saml2:SubjectConfirmation Method="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:cm:bearer">
<saml2:SubjectConfirmationData NotOnOrAfter="2023-08-02T01:18:05.160Z" Recipient="https://sso.security.cisco.com/sso/saml2/0oa1rs8y79aeweVg80h8"/>
</saml2:SubjectConfirmation>
</saml2:Subject>
<saml2:Conditions NotBefore="2023-08-02T01:08:05.160Z" NotOnOrAfter="2023-08-02T01:18:05.160Z">
<saml2:AudienceRestriction>
<saml2:Audience>https://www.okta.com/saml2/service-provider/12345678890</saml2:Audience>
</saml2:AudienceRestriction>
</saml2:Conditions>
<saml2:AuthnStatement AuthnInstant="2023-08-02T01:13:04.861Z">
<saml2:AuthnContext>
<saml2:AuthnContextClassRef>urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:ac:classes:PasswordProtectedTransport</saml2:AuthnContextClassRef>
</saml2:AuthnContext>
</saml2:AuthnStatement>
<saml2:AttributeStatement>
<saml2:Attribute Name="firstName" NameFormat="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:attrname-format:unspecified">
<saml2:AttributeValue
xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"
xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:type="xs:string">Joe
</saml2:AttributeValue>
</saml2:Attribute>
<saml2:Attribute Name="lastName" NameFormat="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:attrname-format:unspecified">
<saml2:AttributeValue
xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"
xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:type="xs:string">Smith
</saml2:AttributeValue>
</saml2:Attribute>
<saml2:Attribute Name="email" NameFormat="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:attrname-format:unspecified">
<saml2:AttributeValue
xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"
xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:type="xs:string">jsmith@example.com
</saml2:AttributeValue>
</saml2:Attribute>
</saml2:AttributeStatement>
</saml2:Assertion>
The attribute
SamlIDPUserGroups
is required to map an identity provider group with a group in an organization.

chunks.heading_text: Example SAML assertion for identity provider group mapping
chunks.body:
<saml2:AttributeStatement xmlns:saml2="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:assertion">
…
<saml2:Attribute Name="SamlIDPUserGroups" NameFormat="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:attrname-format:unspecified">
<saml2:AttributeValue xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"
xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:type="xs:string">IdP_Group_Mapping_1</saml2:AttributeValue>
<saml2:AttributeValue xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"
xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:type="xs:string">IdP_Group_Mapping_2</saml2:AttributeValue>
<saml2:AttributeValue xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"
xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:type="xs:string">452dce15-05fa-4f7c-aa60-30dcefae7433</saml2:AttributeValue>
</saml2:Attribute>
</saml2:AttributeStatement>

To begin, you must provide a name for your Secure Cloud organization, and decide if you want to enroll your users in Duo Multifactor Authentication at no cost, or use your own MFA solution.
For all integrations, Cisco strongly recommends implementing MFA with a session timeout no greater than two hours, to help protect your sensitive data within Cisco Security products.
Review SAML response requirements.

1
2
Choose
Integrations > Identity Providers.
3
Click Add Identity Provider.
If you haven't claimed a domain yet you will instead see an + Add Domain button. Click that button to begin claiming your domain.
4
On the Set up page, enter a name for your identity provider.
5
If needed, opt-out of Duo MFA for users in your claimed domain.
6
Click Next to advance to the Configure page.

chunks.source_title: Step 2: Provide Security Cloud Control SAML metadata to your identity provider
chunks.heading_text: Step 2: Provide Security Cloud Control SAML metadata to your identity provider
chunks.body:
In this step you'll configure your identity provider's SAML application with the SAML metadata and signing certificate provided by Security Cloud Control. This includes the following:
Single Sign-On Service URL – Also called the Assertion Consumer Service (ACS) URL, this is the where your identity provider sends its SAML response after authenticating a user.
Entity ID – Also called Audience URI, this uniquely identifies Security Cloud Sign On to your identity provider.
Signing certificate – The X.509 signing certificate your identity provider uses to verify the signature sent by Security Cloud Sign On in authentication requests.
Security Cloud provides this information in a single SAML metadata file that you can upload

chunks.source_title: Step 2: Provide Security Cloud Control SAML metadata to your identity provider
chunks.heading_text: Step 2: Provide Security Cloud Control SAML metadata to your identity provider
chunks.body:
to your identity provider (if supported), and as individual values, you can copy and paste.
See Identity Service Provider Instructions for steps
specific to several commercially available identity service providers.

chunks.source_title: Step 2: Provide Security Cloud Control SAML metadata to your identity provider
heading_path: Step 2: Provide Security Cloud Control SAML metadata to your identity provider
chunks.body:
1
From the
Identity Providers > Edit identity provider > Configure page, download the SAML metadata file if your identity provider supports it; otherwise, copy the Single Sign-On Service and Entity ID values, and download the Public certificate.

2
On your identity provider, open the SAML application that you want to integrate with Security Cloud Sign On.

3
If supported by your provider, upload the SAML metadata file; otherwise, copy and paste the required Security Cloud Sign On SAML URIs into the corresponding configuration fields in your SAML application, and upload Security Cloud Sign On public signing certificate.

chunks.source_title: Step 2: Provide Security Cloud Control SAML metadata to your identity provider
heading_path: Step 2: Provide Security Cloud Control SAML metadata to your identity provider
chunks.body:
4
Configure your SAML application with the Security Cloud Sign On SAML metadata that you obtained in the previous step, either by importing the XML metadata file or manually entering the SSO Service URL and Entity ID values, and uploading the public signing certificate.

5
Return to Security Cloud Control and click Next.

chunks.source_title: Step 2: Provide Security Cloud Control SAML metadata to your identity provider
chunks.body: Next you'll provide Security Cloud Control with the corresponding metadata for your identity provider's SAML application.

chunks.source_title: Step 3: Provide SAML metadata from your IdP to Security Cloud
chunks.heading_text: Step 3: Provide SAML metadata from your IdP to Security Cloud
chunks.body: After you've configured your identity provider's SAML application with SAML metadata from Security Cloud Control, the next step is to provide the corresponding metadata from your SAML application to Security Cloud Control. See Identity Service Provider Instructions for steps specific to several commercially available identity service providers.

chunks.source_title: Step 3: Provide SAML metadata from your IdP to Security Cloud
chunks.body:
To complete this step, you need the following metadata for the SAML application on your identity provider:
Single Sign-on Service URL
Entity ID (Audience URI)
Signing certificate in PEM format
SamlIDPUserGroups
Depending on how your identity provider provides data, you can either upload a metadata XML
file that contains all this information, or manually enter (copy and paste) the individual
SAML URIs and upload the signing certificate. See Identity Service Provider Instructions for steps
specific to several commercially available identity service providers.

chunks.source_title: Step 3: Provide SAML metadata from your IdP to Security Cloud
chunks.body:
1
In Security Cloud Control, on the
Identity Providers > Edit identity provider > SAML metadata page, do one of the following:
If you have an XML metadata file from your identity provider, select XML file upload and upload the XML file.
Otherwise, click Manual configuration and enter the endpoints for the Single Sign-on Service URL, Entity ID, and upload the public signing certificate provided by your identity provider.
2
Click Next.

chunks.source_title: Step 3: Provide SAML metadata from your IdP to Security Cloud
chunks.body: Next you'll test your integration by initiating an SSO from Security Cloud Control to your identity provider.

After you’ve exchanged SAML metadata between your SAML application and Security Cloud Sign On, you can test the integration. Security Cloud Sign On sends a SAML request to your identity provider’s SSO URL. If your identity provider successfully authenticates the user, they are redirected and automatically signed in to the Application Portal.
Important: Be sure to test with an SSO user account other than the one you used to create the SAML integration in Security Cloud Control. For instance, if you used admin@example.com to create the integration then test with another SSO user (jsmith@example.com, for instance).

1
In Security Cloud Control, from the
Identity Providers > Edit identity provider > Test page, copy the sign in URL to your clipboard and open it in a private (incognito) browser window.
2
Sign in to your identity provider.
The test is successful if, after authenticating with your identity provider, you are signed in to the Application Portal. If you receive an error, see Troubleshooting SAML errors.
Click Next to advance to the Activate step.

After you've tested your SAML integration, you can activate it. Activating an integration has the following effects:
Users in the verified domain must authenticate using the integrated identity provider. If a user tries to sign in using the Cisco or Microsoft social sign-on options, a 400 error occurs.
Users that sign in to Security Cloud
Sign On with an email domain that matches your claimed domain are redirected to your identity provider to authenticate.
If you opted in to Duo MFA, users in your claimed domain can no longer manage their MFA settings.
Be sure to test your integration before activating it.
You can activate an integration in the following way:

1
2
Choose
Integrations > Identity Providers.
3
In the
Identity Providers > Edit identity provider > Activate page, click Activate my IdP.
4
Click Activate in the dialog box to confirm the action.

To enable role-based access control, map the identity provider group to a Security Cloud Control group.
Mapping the identity provider group automatically migrates all users into Security Cloud
Control, eliminating the need to add users manually. You can verify the users in the
Platform Management > Access Management > Administrator Access > Administrators list. To learn how to map, refer to Map identity provider group.

If you get an HTTP 400 error when testing your IdP integration, try the following troubleshooting steps.
Check that the user's sign-on email domain matches the claimed domain
Ensure the email domain of the user account you're using to test matches your claimed domain.
For instance, if you claimed a top-level domain, such as
example.com
, then users must sign in with
<username>@example.com
and not
<username>@signon.example.com
.
Check that the user is signing in through their identity provider
Users must authenticate through the integrated identity provider. An HTTP 400 error is returned if a user signs in using the Cisco or Microsoft social sign-in options or attempts to sign in directly through Okta.
Check that the <NameID> element in the SAML response is an email address
The value of the

<NameId>
element in the SAML response must be an email address. The email address must match the email specified in the user's SAML attributes. See SAML response requirements for details.
Check that the SAML response contains the correct attribute claims
The SAML response from your IdP to Security Cloud Sign On includes the required user attributes: firstName, lastName, and email. See SAML response requirements for details.
Check that the SAML response from your IdP is signed with SHA-256
SAML response from your identity provider must be signed with the SHA-256 signature algorithm. Security Cloud Sign On rejects assertions that are unsigned or signed with another algorithm.

chunks.heading_text: Users do not appear in the federated group
chunks.body:
Check that the SAML response from your IdP contains the group attribute
After integrating your identity provider (IDP) and mapping it to a federated group, if the users are not automatically visible in Security Cloud Control, make sure your IDP sends the SamlIDPUserGroups attribute with the list of IDP groups. See SAML Response Requirements for details.

Check that SAML provider is returning the group attribute
After configuring the domain and Identity Provider (IDP) and linking a
federated group to a managed organization, if the managed organization is not visible, make sure the SAML
provider is returning the group attribute. See SAML Response Requirements for
details.

This chapter provides an overview of role-based access control in Security Cloud Control, and it explains how to create users and groups and assign them permissions to manage
products onboarded to Security Cloud Control.

chunks.source_title: Role-Based Access Control in an Organization
chunks.heading_text: Role-Based Access Control in an Organization
chunks.body:
Security Cloud Control supports role-based access control (RBAC) to automate access management across the organization. A role defines the level of user access to functions within a product. With Security Cloud Control, you can centralize the management of user roles within an organization, allowing organization users to switch seamlessly between products without the need to log in repeatedly.
As an organization administrator, you can define applicable user roles for a product and assign one or more roles to each user. You can organize user accounts into manageable units called groups, which let you assign roles to multiple users simultaneously. Each group can be assigned one or more roles and the members of the group inherit those roles.

chunks.source_title: Role-Based Access Control in an Organization
chunks.heading_text: Role-Based Access Control in an Organization
chunks.body:
The Security Cloud Control user interface offers separate pages to manage administrators, admin groups,
and admin roles.
This document uses the term "users" to refer to the administrators who are the users of Security Cloud Control.
On the Administrators page, you can…
On the Admin groups page, you can…
On the Admin roles page, you can…
Create users
Assign roles to users
Add users to groups
Edit the name of a user
Disable a user account
Restore a user account
View the list of users
Search for a user based on the name, type, or status
User accounts are segregated based on their status:
Current accounts–Displays all active users.
Pending invitations–Displays all users that are invited and whose activations are pending.
Disabled accounts–Displays all those users whose accounts are disabled.

chunks.source_title: Role-Based Access Control in an Organization
chunks.heading_text: Role-Based Access Control in an Organization
chunks.body:
See Managing Users for
more information.
Create a group and add users to the group
Edit a group name
Add or remove users from a group
Assign roles to a group
Delete a group
View the list of groups that are created for the organization
See Managing Groups for more
information.
View the list of roles that are associated with a product
Assign roles to users and groups
Edit or remove roles that are assigned to users or groups
See Managing Roles for more
information.

Organization administrators can invite a user to join an organization.
This task allows you to add new users to groups and assign them roles, making it useful for onboarding new users. A maximum of 20 users can be invited at once using this task.
For a visual walkthrough of the invite process, watch the video Invite a User.

1
2
Choose
Access Management > Administrator Access.

3
Under the Administrators tab, click the +Invite button.

4
In the Add user details pane, provide the first name, last name, and email address of the user.
To proceed to the next step, ensure that you have entered the email address in the correct format and the email address doesn't already exist in the organization.
You can add up to 20 user accounts, using the Add row option.

5
Click Next.
This step enables the Add to groups option.

6
(Optional) In the Add invited users to groups pane, choose a group or groups from the Group(s) drop-down list. This lets you add the invited users to the selected groups.
You can create a new group and add the users to the new group:
To create a new group, click Create new group.
In the Create New Group slide-in pane, enter the group name and description.
Click the Create group button.
The new group is added to the list of groups to which the invited user will be added.

7
Click Next.
This step enables the Assign roles option.

8
In the Assign roles to invited users pane:
Choose a product from the Product or service drop-down list.
To assign the user one or more roles for the selected product, choose a role from the Roles drop-down list.
To add more product roles to the user, click +Assign another role and choose the product and role as you did previously.

9
Click Finish & invite.
The user receives an email invitation to log in to Security Cloud Control.
First-time users also receive an email from Okta, requesting them to provision their Okta account. A user will be able to log in to Security Cloud Control only after being activated in Okta.
On the Administrators page, select Pending invitations from the drop-down list to view the invitations that haven't been activated.
If you have invited a user who already exists in Security Cloud Control, that user appears in the list of Current accounts on the Administrators page.

You can invite multiple users to an organization by importing a file containing user details.
For a visual walkthrough of this import process, watch the video Import Multiple Users.

1
2
Choose
Access Management > Administrator Access.

3
Under the Administrators tab, click Import users.

4
In the Import users from file pane, upload or drag and
drop a .csv file.
You can download the sample example file in CSV format to see the required structure.
In the .csv file, list each user's first name, last name, and email address in a table. Enter each user on a separate row. See the sample table for the required format.
In the table, make sure that the first row serves as the header with the titles "firstName," "lastName," and "email" in that specific order. Subsequent rows must include user information.
Figure 1: Format of .csv file
After the file is successfully imported, the Add to
groups option is enabled.

5
(Optional) From the Group(s) drop-down list, choose the
group or groups to add the invited users.

6
Click Next.
This step enables you to assign roles to the users.

7
In the Assign roles to invited users pane:
Choose a product from the Product or service drop-down list.
This is a mandatory step for Security Cloud Control to assign either a member or administrator role to the user.
To assign the user one or more roles for the selected product, choose a role from the Roles drop-down list.
To add more product roles to the user, click +Assign another role and choose the role and location as you did previously.

8
Click Finish & invite.
The user receives an email invitation to log in to Security Cloud Control.
On the Administrators page, select Pending invitations from the drop-down list to view the invitations that haven't been activated yet.
If you have invited a user who exists in Security Cloud Control, that user appears in the list of Current accounts on the Administrators page.
An organization administrator can create a group and add users to the group.

1
2
Choose
Access Management > Administrator Access.
3
Click Admin groups.
4
Click +Add admin group.
5
Enter the details in the Name and Description fields.
Group name should not exceed 50 characters.
6
Click Next.
7
(Optional) To add users to the group, select them from the Add users drop-down list.
8
Click Finish.
The new group is listed in the Admin Groups page.

An organization administrator manages group memberships, including adding users to groups.
For a visual walkthrough of adding users to a group and assigning roles to a group, watch the video Add Users to Groups and Assign Roles.

1
2
Choose
Access Management > Administrator Access.
3
Click Admin groups.
4
Click the group name to which you want to add users.
5
On the group detail page, click +Add users.
6
In the Add Users slide-in pane, from the drop-down list, choose the users that you want to add to this group.
7
Click Add Users.
The newly added users are listed under the Administrator users section in the group detail page.
Assign roles to the group. All users in the group will inherit the group roles.
An organization administrator can assign one or more roles to users and groups.

1
2
Choose
Access Management > Administrator Access.
3
Click Admin groups.
4
Click the group name to which you want to assign roles.
5
In the Assigned admin roles section, click Assign roles.
6
In the Assign Roles slide-in pane:
A group can have multiple roles assigned for a product.
From the Product or service drop-down list, choose a product.
From the Roles drop-down list, choose one or more roles to assign to the group.
7
Click Assign roles.
The newly assigned roles are listed in the Assigned admin roles section of the group detail page.
After you complete the tasks described in the Getting Started Guide, Security Cloud Control makes some suggestions for what to do next.

In the Security Cloud Control toolbar, click the rocket   to view the Launchpad.
Click the Pending tab to see which security products need
additional configuration. Click the Start button for a
product and read about Security Cloud Control's suggested features and configurations for that product.
If you want to dismiss the suggestion, click the X next to the
Pending badge. The suggestion then moves to the Dismissed
tab.
When you are finished with the suggestion, click the Mark as
Complete link. The task is stored in the
Completed tab in case you want to refer back to it.

On the Security Cloud Control toolbar, click the notification button and look for High Priority firewall notifications and
take appropriate action. You can also set notification preferences.

Go to the Home page . Here are some of the tasks you may want to perform or repeat:
Claim additional product subscriptions.
Onboard additional firewalls or other devices.
Activate security products you've already claimed.
Integrate an identity provider.
Set or reset your default landing page.

Users integrate many different Cisco products with Security Cloud Control. Many of those
integrated products manage other Cisco products. These integrations and managed products
have their own documentation sets. This section provides links to relevant product
documents and documentation summaries.

AI Defense addresses risks for users and providers of AI. Using network visibility and
enforcement points in the Security Cloud Control, AI Defense adds detection and enforcement measures to discover sanctioned and unsanctioned
AI workloads, applications, models, data, and user access across your distributed
cloud environment. For organizations that develop and deliver AI-powered services,
AI Defense detects vulnerabilities in your AI models before they're delivered. For your
running AI applications, AI Defense guardrails intercept rapidly evolving threats,
including prompt injections, denial of service, and data leakage.
See the AI Defense User Guide for detailed
information.

chunks.source_title: Security Cloud Control Firewall Management Documentation
chunks.heading_text: Security Cloud Control Firewall Management Documentation
chunks.body:
Security Cloud Control Firewall Management: Security Cloud Control Firewall Management (formerly Cisco Defense Orchestrator) is a cloud-based security policy manager
that simplifies and unifies policy across your Cisco firewalls and other devices.
See Security Cloud Control Firewall Management
documentation for more information.
This page provides links to documentation that describes features and functionality in
Security Cloud Control Firewall Management.

Multicloud Defense provides a simplified and highly automated approach to multicloud security. This
solution allows organizations to manage and secure their multicloud environments
using a single SaaS delivered control plane, and centralized or distributed
PaaS-delivered data plane architectures. Multicloud Defense provides continuous visibility, unified protection and dynamic policy updates
across all major cloud providers, thereby eliminating the need for separate point
solutions for solutions for each cloud provider.
These documents describe the new features and functionality of Multicloud Defense:
Mulitcloud Control Release Notes
Multicloud Defense User Guide
SASE Management integrates Catalyst SD-WAN and Secure Access into a single, cloud-native management experience integrated with Security Cloud Control.

Existing Catalyst SD-WAN Customers: If you already use Catalyst SD-WAN
Cloud, SASE Management allows you to extend your capabilities with unified
security policy management. For more information, see the Cisco SD-WAN Cloud Guide.
Modifying your organization's subscription model or tier is not supported
once you enable SASE Management.
New Secure Access Customers: New Secure Access Customers: If you are
adopting Secure Access capabilities for the first time, SASE Management
provides an easy-to-use experience that is integrated with SD-WAN Manager.
For more information, see Cisco Secure Access At-a-Glance
and Cisco Secure Access Help.
After a Catalyst SD-WAN organization is linked to SASE Management, it
cannot be detached from SASE Management.

Streamlined onboarding: Easily onboard your Catalyst SD-WAN sites and
devices into the SASE fabric through Security Cloud Control, with flexible deployment options and audit logs to track changes.
Unified policy management: Manage Secure Internet Access (SIA) and
Secure Private Access (SPA) policies for Catalyst SD-WAN and Secure Access devices from one consolidated interface. This reduces complexity and
helps ensure consistent security enforcement across your entire
environment.
Simplified operations: Create, modify, deploy, and monitor policies
without switching between multiple management tools, saving time, and
reducing operational overhead.
Common objects: After SASE Management has been enabled, you can define network objects once and reuse them

across your Catalyst SD-WAN and security policies, improving consistency and
reducing errors.
Integrated troubleshooting: Access device and tunnel health
information and perform basic troubleshooting directly within the unified
platform to quickly resolve issues.
Learn more here:
SASE Management Administration
Guide
Common Objects and Object
Groups

Cisco Secure Access is a cloud-based platform that provides multiple levels of defense against
internet-based threats. Connect securely to the internet, SaaS apps, and private
digital resources from your organization's network or roaming off-network. Using
policy rules, configure and enforce security controls on collections of resources,
users, and devices.
These documents describe the new features and functionality of Secure Access:
Secure Access Release Notes
Secure Access Help

Secure Workload: Cisco Secure Workload (formerly Tetration) seamlessly delivers zero-trust micro-segmentation across any
workload, environment, or location from a single console. With comprehensive
visibility into every workload interaction and powerful AI/ML-driven automation, Secure Workload reduces the attack surface by preventing lateral movement, identifies workload
behavior anomalies, helps rapidly remediate threats, and continuously monitors
policy compliance. See Secure Workload Documentation for more
information.
See Cisco Secure Workload Documentation Roadmaps for more
information.

chunks.heading_text: Browsers Supported by Security Cloud Control
chunks.body:
Security Cloud Control supports the latest version of these browsers.
Google Chrome
Microsoft Edge
Mozilla Firefox

chunks.source_title: Cisco Support Access to your Organization
chunks.heading_text: Cisco Support Access to your Organization
chunks.body:
To help the support team diagnose and debug issues more effectively, you can grant the
team temporary access to your organiztion. This only grants the support team the
platform admin role to view and modify the platform management settings, it will not
give them product roles and the ability to view or edit individual product settings.
This access is automatically revoked after a specified duration, and can also be
disabled anytime after it is no longer needed.

Follow this procedure to manually enable access to your organization by Cisco
Support. Access automatically expires after your specified period ends.

1
Click Enable support access and choose the access
duration.
2
(Optional) Copy the organization ID to provide to Cisco support.
3
Click Enable.
4
Click Enable access.
You see a message explaining when access expires.

Follow this procedure to manually disable access to your organization by Cisco
Support.

1
Click Disable support access and choose the access
duration.
2
Click Disable.
3
Click Disable access.

These are methods of contacting the Cisco Technical Assistance Center (TAC):
Use the Support Case Manager: https://mycase.cloudapps.cisco.com/case
Email Cisco TAC: tac@cisco.com
Call Cisco TAC (North America): 1.408.526.7209 or 1.800.553.2447
Call Cisco TAC (worldwide):
Cisco Worldwide Support Contacts
This chapter describes how to allow Cisco Support to access your organization to help troubleshoot any issues you may have.
To grant the support team access to your organization for better diagnosis and debugging, perform this procedure.

1
In the Security Cloud Control window, hover your cursor over the Organization menu and, from the slide-in pane, select an organization.
The Security Cloud Control page reloads with the selected organization.

2
3
Choose
Settings > Support Access.

4
Click Enable support access.

5
In the Enable Support Access slide-in pane, select the duration from the Access duration drop-down list.
This is the duration for which the Cisco Support team has access to your organization account.

6
Click the clipboard icon to copy the identifier (ID) of the organization.
Paste the ID into a safe text tool.
When asked, provide the ID to the Cisco Support team.

7
Click Enable.
Access to your organization is enabled and the support team can access your organization for the duration that you have configured. At the end of this period, access is automatically revoked.
